title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: cliomicplot R package creating high-quality, publication-ready visualizations clinical multi-omics data analysis. Built ggplot2, provides 16 specialized plot types, 10 journal-specific themes, 55+ color palettes — clean, formula-based API. vignette walks core concepts basic usage.

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
code: # From source remotes::install_local("path/to/cliomicplot")  # Dependencies install.packages(c(   "ggplot2", "scales", "survival", "survminer",   "RColorBrewer", "reshape2", "ggrepel",   "patchwork", "ggpubr", "ggtext", "circlize" )) library(cliomicplot) #> cliomicplot 0.1.0 - Publication-ready clinical & omics plots #> Main function: cliplot() | Themes: clitheme() | Params: clipar()

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: cliomicplot uses formula-based API: y ~ x | group.  | group part optional. Every plot automatically gets: Proper axis labels derived variable names legend grouping present color palette applied groups
code: # Basic scatter with grouping cliplot(Sepal.Length ~ Petal.Length | Species, data = iris)

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: Pass type = "..." type_*() function:  type omitted, cliomicplot guesses based data types:
code: # Boxplot with built-in statistical test cliplot(Sepal.Length ~ Species, data = iris,         type = "boxplot",         stat.test = "kruskal.test",         stat.label = "p.signif",         palette = "jco")

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: Built-statistical test support — extra packages needed:  Supported tests: "t.test", "wilcox.test", "anova", "kruskal.test". Supported label formats: "p.format" (e.g., p = 0.023), "p.signif" (e.g., *, **), "p.adj".
code: # t-test with p-value formatting cliplot(len ~ dose, data = ToothGrowth,         type = "boxplot",         stat.test = "t.test",         stat.label = "p.format",         palette = "jco") #> Warning: Orientation is not uniquely specified when both the x and y aesthetics are #> continuous. Picking default orientation 'x'. #> Warning: Continuous x aesthetic #> ℹ did you forget `aes(group = ...)`?

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: Set theme applies subsequent plots:
code: clitheme("nature") #> Theme set to: nature cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars) # The theme persists cliplot(len ~ dose, data = ToothGrowth, type = "boxplot") #> Warning: Orientation is not uniquely specified when both the x and y aesthetics are #> continuous. Picking default orientation 'x'. #> Warning: Continuous x aesthetic #> ℹ did you forget `aes(group = ...)`? clitheme()  # reset to default

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: Apply theme one plot :
code: cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars, theme = "nature") cliplot(Sepal.Length ~ Species, data = iris, type = "boxplot", theme = "dark")

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: cliomicplot ships 55+ palettes unified system:
code: # List all palettes (first 20 shown) head(cli_palette_list(), 20) #>  [1] "jco"            "nejm"           "lancet"         "nature"         #>  [5] "science"        "okabe_ito"      "tableau10"      "tol_muted"      #>  [9] "heatmap_rdbu"   "heatmap_rdylbu" "heatmap_prgn"   "volcano"        #> [13] "pastel"         "soft"           "neon"           "cyberpunk"      #> [17] "blues"          "reds"           "greens"         "purples" # Use any palette by name cliplot(Sepal.Length ~ Petal.Length | Species, data = iris,         palette = "jama") cliplot(Sepal.Length ~ Petal.Length | Species, data = iris,         palette = "cosmic")

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: palettes work standalone scales ggplot:
code: library(ggplot2) #> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.3  ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   palette_scale("npg", "color") +   theme_bw()

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: Add bold, italic, colored, subscript/superscript text labels:
code: cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars, title = "**Mileage** vs *Weight* in mtcars", subtitle = "n = 32 vehicles, 1974 Motor Trend", caption = "*Motor Trend* (1974)") + cli_markdown()

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
code: # Only the title renders markdown cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars,         title = "**Bold Title**",         xlab  = "Weight (1000 lbs)",    # plain text         ylab  = "Miles / Gallon") +      # plain text   cli_markdown("title")

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: Set defaults apply subsequent plots:
code: # View current parameters (first 10) names(clipar())[1:10] #>  [1] "facet.font"        "font.cap"          "stat.test"         #>  [4] "facet.border"      "facet.cex"         "palette.diverging" #>  [7] "file.width"        "theme.default"     "legend.direction"  #> [10] "cex.sub" # Set defaults clipar(palette.qualitative = "nejm") clipar(stat.test = "wilcox.test") clipar(file.width = 8, file.height = 6, file.res = 600)  # Query clipar("palette.qualitative") #> [1] "nejm"

title: Getting Started with cliomicplot
text: Save plots directly cliplot() one call. Supports pdf, png, jpg, svg, tiff: Set global defaults file output:
code: cliplot(Sepal.Length ~ Petal.Length | Species, data = iris,         file   = "iris_scatter.pdf",         width  = 6,         height = 5)  # PNG with high DPI cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars,         file = "mpg_wt.png", width = 8, height = 6) clipar(file.width = 8, file.height = 6, file.res = 600) cliplot(Sepal.Length ~ Petal.Length | Species, data = iris,         file = "fig1_highres.pdf")

title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
text: vignette demonstrates multi-omics exploratory analysis workflow using cliomicplot. ’ll generate four figures commonly found omics publications: Figure 1 — PCA confidence ellipses Figure 2 — Correlation heatmap clustering significance Figure 3 — Volcano plot differential expression Figure 4 — MA plot DE quality assessment
code: library(cliomicplot) #> cliomicplot 0.1.0 - Publication-ready clinical & omics plots #> Main function: cliplot() | Themes: clitheme() | Params: clipar() clipar(stat.test = NULL)  # disable auto stat annotations

title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
text: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) workhorse omics data exploration. cliomicplot can compute PCA raw data accept pre-computed results:

title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
text: axis labels automatically include variance explained percentages, confidence ellipses drawn around group.
code: clitheme("nature") #> Theme set to: nature  cliplot(iris[, 1:4],         type = type_pca(           pc_x          = 1,           pc_y          = 2,           center        = TRUE,           scale.        = TRUE,           add_ellipse   = TRUE,           ellipse_level = 0.95,           point_size    = 2.5,           label_samples = FALSE         ),         by       = iris$Species,         palette  = "npg",         stat.test = NULL,         title    = "**Figure 1** — PCA of Iris Morphometric Data",         subtitle = "PC1 (72.9%) vs. PC2 (22.9%), 95% CI ellipses") +   cli_markdown()

title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
text: already PCA results (e.g., prcomp(), DESeq2, another pipeline), cliomicplot accepts scores directly:
code: pca_res <- prcomp(iris[, 1:4], center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE) scores  <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x) scores$Species <- iris$Species  cliplot(PC2 ~ PC1, data = scores, by = scores$Species,         type      = "points",         stat.test = NULL,         palette   = "d3_category10",         title     = "**PCA Scores** — Pre-computed by prcomp()",         theme     = "science") +   cli_markdown()

title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
code: cliplot(iris[, 1:4],         type = type_pca(           add_ellipse   = FALSE,           label_samples = TRUE,           label_size    = 2.5,           point_size    = 2         ),         by      = iris$Species,         palette = "jco",         stat.test = NULL,         title   = "**PCA with Sample Labels**") +   cli_markdown()

title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
text: volcano plot canonical visualization differential expression analysis. ’ll generate realistic simulated data:
code: set.seed(42)  # Generate 8000 "genes" — most are null n_genes <- 8000 de_data <- data.frame(   logFC   = rnorm(n_genes, 0, 0.7),   PValue  = 10^-rnorm(n_genes, 2.5, 1.8),   row.names = paste0("GENE", 1:n_genes) )  # Inject 200 "true" DE genes sig_idx <- sample(1:n_genes, 200) de_data$logFC[sig_idx]  <- rnorm(200, ifelse(runif(200) > 0.5, 2.0, -2.0), 0.5) de_data$PValue[sig_idx] <- 10^-runif(200, 5, 15)  cat(sprintf("Genes tested: %d\n", n_genes)) #> Genes tested: 8000 cat(sprintf("DE genes (|log2FC| > 1, p < 0.05): %d\n",             sum(abs(de_data$logFC) > 1 & de_data$PValue < 0.05))) #> DE genes (|log2FC| > 1, p < 0.05): 1081 clitheme("cell") #> Theme set to: cell  cliplot(-log10(PValue) ~ logFC, data = de_data,         type = type_volcano(           pval_cutoff  = 0.01,           fc_cutoff    = 1,           label_genes  = "significant",           max_overlaps = 25,           point_alpha  = 0.5         ),         title    = "**Figure 3** — Volcano Plot of Differential Expression",         subtitle = "FDR < 0.01, |log<sub>2</sub>FC| > 1",         caption  = paste0("Top labeled genes; ", n_genes, " probes tested")) +   cli_markdown()

title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
code: # Tighter thresholds, different colors cliplot(-log10(PValue) ~ logFC, data = de_data,         type = type_volcano(           pval_cutoff  = 0.001,           fc_cutoff    = 1.5,           label_genes  = NULL,       # no labels           up_color     = "#BC3C29",  # NEJM red           down_color   = "#0072B5",  # NEJM blue           ns_color     = "grey85",           point_size   = 1.5,           point_alpha  = 0.4         ),         palette = "volcano",         title   = "**Volcano Plot** — Stringent Thresholds",         subtitle = "p < 0.001, |log<sub>2</sub>FC| > 1.5") +   cli_markdown()

what: Figure 4: MA Plot — DE Quality Check
title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
text: MA (Mean-Average) plot complements volcano showing relationship fold-change mean expression level:  MA plot shows: - y-axis: log⁡2\log_2 fold change (biological effect size) - x-axis: Mean normalized counts (expression level, log₁₀ scale) - Red points: Significant DE genes - Blue curve: LOESS trend line (checks intensity-dependent bias) - Horizontal dashed line: log⁡2FC=0\log_2\text{FC} = 0 (change)
code: set.seed(123) ma_data <- data.frame(   baseMean       = 10^runif(3000, 0, 5),   log2FoldChange = rnorm(3000, 0, 0.6),   padj           = runif(3000) ) # Add "real" DE genes ma_data$log2FoldChange[sample(1:3000, 150)] <- rnorm(150, 2, 0.5) ma_data$log2FoldChange[sample(1:3000, 150)] <- rnorm(150, -2, 0.5)  clitheme("science") #> Theme set to: science  cliplot(log2FoldChange ~ baseMean, data = ma_data,         type = type_ma(           pval_cutoff = 0.05,           add_loess   = TRUE,           loess_color = "#3C5488",           sig_color   = "#E64B35",           ns_color    = "grey70",           point_size  = 1.2,           point_alpha = 0.4         ),         title    = "**Figure 4** — MA Plot",         subtitle = "DE genes highlighted (padj < 0.05), LOESS trend") +   cli_markdown() #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
text: completeness, ’s clustered heatmap workflow:  Note: heatmap uses ComplexHeatmap available, falling back pheatmap. Install install.packages("ComplexHeatmap") best experience.
code: set.seed(789) expr_mat <- matrix(rnorm(500), nrow = 50) rownames(expr_mat) <- paste0("Gene_", 1:50) colnames(expr_mat) <- paste0("Sample_", sprintf("%02d", 1:10))  # Column annotations ann_col <- data.frame(   Condition = rep(c("Control", "Treatment"), each = 5),   Batch     = rep(c("A", "B"), 5),   row.names = colnames(expr_mat) )  # Annotation color mapping ann_colors <- list(   Condition = c(Control = "#4DBBD5", Treatment = "#E64B35"),   Batch     = c(A = "#E18727", B = "#3C5488") ) cliplot(expr_mat,         type = type_heatmap(           scale             = "row",           cluster_rows      = TRUE,           cluster_cols      = TRUE,           annotation_col    = ann_col,           annotation_colors = ann_colors,           color_low         = "#4575B4",           color_mid         = "white",           color_high        = "#D73027",           fontsize          = 9         ))

what: Figure 5: Infographic Bar Chart — Results Comparison
title: Multi-Omics Exploratory Analysis with cliomicplot
text: type_infobar() creates media-ready bar charts per-category custom colours, footer boxes, reference lines. Use polling, survey results, side--side comparison category carries semantic colouring:
code: infobar_data <- data.frame(   endpoint  = c("ORR", "DCR", "PFS6", "OS12", "AE3"),   value     = c(42, 68, 55, 72, 12),   bar_color = c("#0073C2", "#4DBBD5", "#00A087", "#3C5488", "#E64B35"),   box_color = c("#005A9E", "#3AA8C2", "#00806A", "#2A3E6E", "#C9302C"),   group     = c("Efficacy", "Efficacy", "Efficacy", "Efficacy", "Safety"),   change    = c("(+3)", "(+5)", "(-1)", "(+2)", "(-4)"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  cliplot(value ~ endpoint, data = infobar_data,         type = type_infobar(           bar_colors      = setNames(infobar_data$bar_color,                                      infobar_data$endpoint),           box_colors      = setNames(infobar_data$box_color,                                      infobar_data$endpoint),           box_labels      = infobar_data$endpoint,           box_sub_labels  = infobar_data$group,           change_labels   = infobar_data$change,           reference_line  = 40,           reference_label = "40%",           value_suffix    = "%",           bar_width       = 0.82         ),         title    = "**Figure 5** — Efficacy & Safety Endpoint Comparison",         subtitle = "Clinical trial results by endpoint category")

title: End-to-End Oncology Analysis with cliomicplot
text: vignette demonstrates complete oncology clinical data analysis workflow using cliomicplot. ’ll generate four publication-quality figures commonly found oncology manuscripts: Figure 1 — Kaplan-Meier overall survival curves Figure 2 — Forest plot multivariable Cox regression Figure 3A — Waterfall plot best tumor response Figure 3B — Swimmer plot patient treatment courses
code: library(cliomicplot) #> cliomicplot 0.1.0 - Publication-ready clinical & omics plots #> Main function: cliplot() | Themes: clitheme() | Params: clipar() library(survival)  # Set global defaults for this analysis clipar(   palette.qualitative = "nejm",   stat.test           = NULL,   file.res            = 600 )

title: End-to-End Oncology Analysis with cliomicplot
text: use lung dataset survival package visualize overall survival stratified sex.
code: clitheme("nejm") #> Theme set to: nejm  cliplot(Surv(time, status) ~ sex, data = lung,         type = type_km(           risk_table  = TRUE,           pval        = TRUE,           conf_int    = TRUE,           median_line = TRUE         ),         palette  = "nejm",         title    = "**Figure 1** — Overall Survival by Sex",         subtitle = "NCCTG Lung Cancer Trial (n = 228)",         xlab     = "Time (days)",         ylab     = "Survival Probability",         legend   = "top") +   cli_markdown() #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ggpubr package. #>   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/kassambara/ggpubr/issues>. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated. #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Ignoring unknown labels: #> • colour : ""

what: Figure 2: Forest Plot — Multivariable Cox Regression
title: End-to-End Oncology Analysis with cliomicplot
text: Fit Cox proportional hazards model visualize hazard ratios:
code: # Fit Cox model cox_fit <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ age + sex + ph.ecog + wt.loss, data = lung) cox_sum <- summary(cox_fit)  # Extract results into a forest-plot-ready data frame forest_df <- data.frame(   Variable = c("Age (per year)", "Male vs. Female",                "ECOG PS (per point)", "Weight loss (per lb)"),   HR       = cox_sum$conf.int[, "exp(coef)"],   CI_low   = cox_sum$conf.int[, "lower .95"],   CI_high  = cox_sum$conf.int[, "upper .95"],   P        = cox_sum$coefficients[, "Pr(>|z|)"] )  forest_df #>                     Variable        HR    CI_low   CI_high            P #> age           Age (per year) 1.0134589 0.9945143 1.0327643 1.649509e-01 #> sex          Male vs. Female 0.5538981 0.3928084 0.7810504 7.535384e-04 #> ph.ecog  ECOG PS (per point) 1.6738244 1.3075807 2.1426504 4.340524e-05 #> wt.loss Weight loss (per lb) 0.9910344 0.9781867 1.0040508 1.761353e-01 clitheme("lancet") #> Theme set to: lancet  cliplot(HR ~ Variable, data = forest_df,         type = type_forest(           ref_line   = TRUE,           sort       = TRUE,           point_size = 4,           ci_width   = 1.2,           sig_color  = "#E64B35",           ns_color   = "#3C5488"         ),         title    = "**Figure 2** — Multivariable Cox Regression",         subtitle = "Overall Survival — Hazard Ratios (95% CI)",         caption  = "Lung Cancer Dataset (n = 228)") +   cli_markdown() #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: End-to-End Oncology Analysis with cliomicplot
text: Generate simulated immunotherapy trial dataset visualize best tumor response:
code: set.seed(42) n_pts <- 30  imtrial <- data.frame(   Patient    = paste0("IM-", sprintf("%03d", 1:n_pts)),   BestChange = sort(rnorm(n_pts, mean = -22, sd = 32)),   BestResp   = sample(c("CR", "PR", "SD", "PD"), n_pts,                       replace = TRUE,                       prob = c(0.07, 0.30, 0.36, 0.27)),   Duration   = pmax(1, round(rnorm(n_pts, mean = 14, sd = 7), 1)),   Arm        = sample(c("Anti-PD1", "Chemotherapy"), n_pts, replace = TRUE) )  # Show first few rows head(imtrial) #>   Patient BestChange BestResp Duration          Arm #> 1  IM-001 -107.00657       PD     17.0 Chemotherapy #> 2  IM-002 -100.09494       CR      8.3 Chemotherapy #> 3  IM-003  -79.00187       PD     24.1     Anti-PD1 #> 4  IM-004  -78.42122       PR     11.0     Anti-PD1 #> 5  IM-005  -66.44354       PD     18.6     Anti-PD1 #> 6  IM-006  -42.47984       SD     16.3     Anti-PD1 clitheme("broadsheet") #> Theme set to: broadsheet  cliplot(BestChange ~ Patient, data = imtrial,         type = type_waterfall(           show_labels = TRUE,           label_size  = 2.5,           bar_width   = 0.75,           response_thresholds = c("PD" = 20, "SD" = -30)         ),         title    = "**Figure 3A** — Best Tumor Response (Waterfall)",         subtitle = "RECIST v1.1 — % Change from Baseline",         ylab     = "Best % Change from Baseline",         legend   = "bottom") +   cli_markdown()

title: End-to-End Oncology Analysis with cliomicplot
text: Add clinical event markers patient timeline:
code: set.seed(123) n_events_per_pt <- sample(1:3, n_pts, replace = TRUE) n_total_events  <- sum(n_events_per_pt)  events_df <- data.frame(   ID    = rep(imtrial$Patient, times = n_events_per_pt),   Time  = round(runif(n_total_events, 0, 28), 1),   Event = sample(c("Progression", "AE Gr≥3", "Dose Reduction", "Response"),                  n_total_events,                  replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.3, 0.25, 0.2, 0.25)) )  head(events_df) #>       ID Time          Event #> 1 IM-001 13.4        AE Gr≥3 #> 2 IM-001 21.2        AE Gr≥3 #> 3 IM-001  6.1       Response #> 4 IM-002  8.9        AE Gr≥3 #> 5 IM-002  6.5        AE Gr≥3 #> 6 IM-002  4.0 Dose Reduction cliplot(Duration ~ Patient, data = imtrial,         type = type_swimmer(           bar_fill    = "BestResp",           event_times = events_df,           bar_alpha   = 0.85         ),         palette = "jco",         title   = "**Figure 3B** — Patient Treatment Course (Swimmer)",         subtitle = "Duration on study with key clinical events",         xlab    = "",         ylab    = "Time on Study (months)",         legend  = "bottom") +   cli_markdown() #> Warning: Removed 21 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: End-to-End Oncology Analysis with cliomicplot
text: Use patchwork combine waterfall swimmer plots:

code: # Simulated trial pipeline data pipeline <- data.frame(   company = rep(c("AstraZeneca", "Roche", "MSD", "Lilly", "Hengrui",                   "Sanofi", "Novartis", "BMS", "J&J", "Pfizer", "AbbVie"),                 each = 5),   phase   = rep(c("I", "I/II", "II", "II/III", "III"), 11),   n       = c(4,3,24,0,32, 10,8,21,0,19, 4,2,30,1,15, 6,3,15,0,17,               15,0,15,0,8, 7,0,14,0,16, 8,3,7,0,17, 7,5,14,1,6,               6,2,7,0,14, 9,2,10,0,6, 4,3,4,0,15) )  phase_colors <- c(   "I"      = "#F8D95C", "I/II"   = "#D69B3C", "II"     = "#A7BA91",   "II/III" = "#B76348", "III"    = "#123F2A" ) phase_text <- c(   "I"      = "#173E2B", "I/II"   = "#173E2B", "II"     = "#173E2B",   "II/III" = "white",   "III"    = "#F6F1DE" )  cliplot(n ~ company | phase, data = pipeline,         type = type_trials(           segment_colors      = phase_colors,           segment_text_colors = phase_text,           label_threshold     = 3         ),         title    = "**Figure 4** — Industry-Sponsored Clinical Trials by Phase",         subtitle = "Companies with ≥ 25 completed trials, 2025",         caption  = "Source: Citeline Annual Completed Clinical Trials Report")

title: Themes and Palettes in cliomicplot
code: # ── Persistent: applies to ALL subsequent plots ──────────────────── clitheme("nature") #> Theme set to: nature  cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars,         title = "Plot 1 — Nature theme persists") cliplot(len ~ dose, data = ToothGrowth, type = "boxplot",         title = "Plot 2 — Still Nature theme") #> Warning: Orientation is not uniquely specified when both the x and y aesthetics are #> continuous. Picking default orientation 'x'. #> Warning: Continuous x aesthetic #> ℹ did you forget `aes(group = ...)`? clitheme()  # ← reset to default # ── Ephemeral: applies to ONE plot only ──────────────────────────── cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars, theme = "nature",         title = "This plot: Nature") cliplot(Sepal.Length ~ Species, data = iris, type = "boxplot",         theme = "dark",         title = "This plot: Dark") cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars,         title = "Back to default — cli_bw")

title: Themes and Palettes in cliomicplot
text: Register themes reuse across projects:
code: # Register a custom theme clitheme_register("my_submission",   base = "cli_bw",   axis.text     = ggplot2::element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),   plot.title    = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),   plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, color = "grey40"),   legend.position   = "bottom",   legend.background = ggplot2::element_rect(color = "grey80") ) #> Theme registered: my_submission  # Use it clitheme("my_submission") #> Theme set to registered theme: my_submission cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars,         title = "Custom 'my_submission' Theme",         subtitle = "12pt bold text, centered") clitheme() # List all themes (including custom ones) clitheme_list() #>  [1] "cli_bw"        "cli_classic"   "cli_minimal"   "nature"        #>  [5] "science"       "nejm"          "lancet"        "cell"          #>  [9] "dark"          "showcase"      "broadsheet"    "my_submission"  # Remove a custom theme clitheme_unregister("my_submission") #> Theme unregistered: my_submission

title: Volcano Plots — from Quick to Publication-Ready
text: volcano plot canonical visualisation differential-expression analysis. vignette shows one-liner cliplot() dives customisation — adjusted p-values, flexible fold-change thresholds, gene labelling, rich annotation call-outs — type_volcano() constructor.

title: Volcano Plots — from Quick to Publication-Ready
text: Replace section DESeq2 / edgeR / limma output table. data. Columns must include log2 fold-change, (preferably adjusted) p-value, optional gene identifiers row.names.
code: set.seed(2026)  de_results <- data.frame(   gene    = paste0("Gene_", 1:1000),   log2FC  = rnorm(1000, mean = 0, sd = 0.7),   pvalue  = runif(1000, 0.00001, 1),   padj    = p.adjust(runif(1000, 0.00001, 1), method = "BH"),   row.names = paste0("Gene_", 1:1000) )  # Inject a strongly significant hit de_results <- rbind(   de_results,   data.frame(     gene    = "CEACAM5",     log2FC  = 2.79,     pvalue  = 0.0001,     padj    = 0.0027,     row.names = "CEACAM5"   ) )  head(de_results[, c("gene","log2FC","pvalue","padj")]) #>          gene      log2FC      pvalue      padj #> Gene_1 Gene_1  0.36441235 0.961683824 0.9482873 #> Gene_2 Gene_2 -0.75578353 0.250218781 0.9889257 #> Gene_3 Gene_3  0.09746668 0.816042035 0.9482873 #> Gene_4 Gene_4 -0.05932415 0.543788992 0.9482873 #> Gene_5 Gene_5 -0.46664773 0.908309869 0.9482873 #> Gene_6 Gene_6 -1.76126232 0.007981887 0.9713784

title: Volcano Plots — from Quick to Publication-Ready
text: single call produces: Points coloured significance class (/ / NS) Dashed threshold lines chosen p-value fold-change cutoffs Automatic ggrepel labels every gene passing thresholds journal-style theme curated palette
code: cliplot(-log10(padj) ~ log2FC, data = de_results,         type  = type_volcano(           pval_cutoff  = 0.05,           fc_cutoff    = 0.25,           point_alpha  = 0.65,           label_genes  = "significant",           max_overlaps = 20         ),         palette = "nature",         theme   = "nature",         title   = "Differential expression — quick volcano",         subtitle = expression(FDR < 0.05 * ", |" * log[2] * "FC| > 0.25"))

title: Volcano Plots — from Quick to Publication-Ready
text: Every visual element exposed. tighten thresholds, change colour mapping, add shaded significance region, label top 15 hits adjusted p-value.
code: # Identify top-15 significant genes sig_idx <- order(de_results$padj)[1:15] top_genes <- rownames(de_results)[sig_idx]  cliplot(-log10(padj) ~ log2FC, data = de_results,         type = type_volcano(           pval_cutoff    = 0.01,           fc_cutoff      = 0.50,           up_color       = "#C95C43",           down_color     = "#4C8BA8",           ns_color       = "#B8B8B8",           point_alpha    = 0.62,           label_genes    = top_genes,           max_overlaps   = 30,           cutoff_region_alpha = 0.04,           show_threshold_labels = TRUE         ),         palette = "jco",         title   = "Stringent thresholds — top 15 genes labelled",         subtitle = expression(FDR < 0.01 * ", |" * log[2] * "FC| > 0.50"))

title: Volcano Plots — from Quick to Publication-Ready
text: publication figure often want one two genes highlighted detailed annotation box reporting fold-change, confidence interval, exact p-values. type_volcano works plain geom_label_repel; can layer richer annotation top using ggtext::geom_richtext.
code:
# Build annotation for CEACAM5 highlight <- de_results[rownames(de_results) == "CEACAM5", ] highlight$ci_low <- 1.86 highlight$ci_high <- 3.73 highlight$annot <- paste0( "", highlight$gene, "", "

", "Fold change = ", round(highlight$log2FC, 2), "", " (95% CI: ", highlight$ci_low, ", ", highlight$ci_high, ")", "
", "Unadjusted P-value < 0.0001", "
", "Adjusted P-value = ", format(highlight$padj, digits = 2), "" ) # Base volcano from cliplot, then layer annotation p_base <- cliplot(-log10(padj) ~ log2FC, data = de_results, type = type_volcano( pval_cutoff = 0.05, fc_cutoff = 0.25, label_genes = "significant", max_overlaps = 15, point_alpha = 0.60 ), palette = "nature", theme = "nature", title = "Volcano plot with gene annotation call-out", subtitle = expression(FDR < 0.05 * ", |" * log[2] * "FC| > 0.25")) # Layer highlight + annotation on top of the returned ggplot p_annot <- p_base + ggplot2::geom_point( data = highlight, ggplot2::aes(x = log2FC, y = -log10(padj)), inherit.aes = FALSE, shape = 21, size = 5, stroke = 1.4, fill = "#C95C43", colour = "black" ) + ggtext::geom_richtext( data = highlight, ggplot2::aes(x = log2FC - 1.8, y = -log10(padj) + 1.8, label = annot), inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5, size = 3.5, fill = "#F9E5D5", colour = "black", label.colour = NA, label.padding = grid::unit(c(8, 10, 8, 10), "pt") ) + ggplot2::geom_segment( data = highlight, ggplot2::aes(x = log2FC, y = -log10(padj), xend = log2FC - 0.3, yend = -log10(padj) + 0.55), inherit.aes = FALSE, linewidth = 0.5, colour = "#555555" ) p_annot

title: Volcano Plots — from Quick to Publication-Ready
text: differential-expression pipeline (DESeq2, edgeR, limma, …) produces table least columns: Row names hold gene / probe identifiers. Tip. adjusted p-value column contains exact zeros (common DESeq2 highly significant genes), cap calling cliplot:
code: # Example: loading DESeq2 output res <- read.csv("differential_expression_results.csv") rownames(res) <- res$gene_symbol  cliplot(-log10(padj) ~ log2FC, data = res,         type = type_volcano(           pval_cutoff  = 0.05,           fc_cutoff    = 0.25,           label_genes  = "significant",           max_overlaps = 20         ),         title = "Differential expression results") res$padj <- pmax(res$padj, 1e-300)

title: Volcano Plots — from Quick to Publication-Ready
code: # High-resolution PNG for manuscript cliplot(-log10(padj) ~ log2FC, data = de_results,         type = type_volcano(),         theme = "nature", palette = "nature",         file = "fig_volcano.png", width = 9, height = 7)  # Vector PDF for journal submission cliplot(-log10(padj) ~ log2FC, data = de_results,         type = type_volcano(),         theme = "nature", palette = "nature",         file = "fig_volcano.pdf", width = 9, height = 7)

what: Publication-Ready Plots for Clinical and Multi-Omics Data — cliplot
title: Publication-Ready Plots for Clinical and Multi-Omics Data — cliplot
text: Main plotting function creating publication-ready visualizations. Supports formula interface automatic grouping, faceting, journal-specific themes. Built ggplot2.

title: Publication-Ready Plots for Clinical and Multi-Omics Data — cliplot
code: cliplot(x, ...)  # Default S3 method cliplot(   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   ...,   data = NULL,   type = NULL,   by = NULL,   facet = NULL,   palette = NULL,   theme = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   legend = NULL,   add = FALSE,   stat.test = NULL,   stat.label = NULL,   file = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   km_time = NULL,   km_event = NULL,   km_group = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'formula' cliplot(   x,   data = NULL,   ...,   type = NULL,   by = NULL,   facet = NULL,   palette = NULL,   theme = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   legend = NULL,   add = FALSE,   stat.test = NULL,   stat.label = NULL,   file = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' cliplot(   x,   ...,   formula = NULL,   type = NULL,   by = NULL,   facet = NULL,   palette = NULL,   theme = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   legend = NULL,   stat.test = NULL,   file = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'matrix' cliplot(   x,   ...,   type = NULL,   theme = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   file = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL )

title: Publication-Ready Plots for Clinical and Multi-Omics Data — cliplot
text: x, y x y variables. Can vectors, data frame columns, specified via formula. ... Additional arguments passed plot type's draw function. data data.frame containing variables. type Plot type. Can character string ("points", "boxplot", "violin", "histogram", "density", "barplot", "lines", "volcano", "forest", "km", "waterfall", "swimmer", "heatmap", "pca", "ma", "correlation") call type_*() function customization. Grouping variable. Alternative | formula. facet Faceting variable(s) formula. One-sided formulas (e.g. ~ group) use facet_wrap. Two-sided formulas (e.g. rows ~ cols) (e.g. panel ~ logfc + fdr) use facet_grid. palette Color palette name vector colors. Built-options: "jco", "nejm", "lancet", "nature", "science", "okabe_ito". theme Theme name ggplot2 theme object. See clitheme. title Plot title. subtitle Plot subtitle. caption Plot caption. xlab, ylab Axis labels. legend Legend position ("right", "left", "top", "bottom", "none"). add Logical. TRUE, adds existing cliplot. yet implemented. stat.test Statistical test comparisons. One "wilcox.test", "t.test", "kruskal.test", "anova", NULL. stat.label Format statistical labels. "p.format", "p.signif", "p.adj". file Optional file path save plot (pdf, png, jpg, svg). width, height Plot dimensions inches. km_time, km_event, km_group Internal. Column names extracted Surv() formula Kaplan-Meier plots; set automatically formula method intended supplied directly. formula formula form y ~ x | group ~ x | group one-sided plots. grouping variable | optional.

what: Set or Reset Plot Themes for cliomicplot — clitheme
title: Set or Reset Plot Themes for cliomicplot — clitheme
text: Sets resets theme cliomicplot plots. Themes control overall appearance including fonts, grid lines, axis styles, colors. Inspired tinyplot's `tinytheme()`, themes can persistent (applied globally) ephemeral (single plot call).

title: Set or Reset Plot Themes for cliomicplot — clitheme
code: clitheme(theme = NULL, ..., register = NULL)

title: Set or Reset Plot Themes for cliomicplot — clitheme
text: theme Character string specifying theme name. Available themes: "cli_bw" - Clean black--white theme (default) "cli_classic" - Classic theme axis lines, panel border "cli_minimal" - Minimal theme, clean modern "nature" - Nature Publishing Group style "science" - Science/AAAS journal style "nejm" - NEJM (New England Journal Medicine) style "lancet" - Lancet style "cell" - Cell Press style "broadsheet" - Publication print style subtle grid "showcase" - Polished presentation style stronger hierarchy "dark" - Dark background theme Use clitheme() without arguments reset default theme. ... Named arguments override specific theme settings (passed ggplot2's theme()). register Optional character string register theme custom name via clitheme_register.

title: Set or Reset Plot Themes for cliomicplot — clitheme
text: **Persistent vs. ephemeral themes.** Calling clitheme("nature") triggers persistent theme applied subsequent ggplot2 plots. Reset calling clitheme() without arguments. ephemeral (single-plot) themes, pass theme = "nature" directly cliplot.

title: Set or Reset Plot Themes for cliomicplot — clitheme
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Set a persistent theme clitheme("nature") cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)  # Reset to default clitheme()  # Ephemeral theme for one plot cliplot(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars, theme = "dark") } # }

what: Enable Markdown Rendering in Plot Text Elements — cli_markdown
title: Enable Markdown Rendering in Plot Text Elements — cli_markdown
text: Selectively enables markdown rendering specific ggplot2 text elements using `ggtext::element_markdown()`. Unlike blanket theme converter, lets target exactly labels interpret markdown (e.g., title subtitle, leaving axis labels plain text).

title: Enable Markdown Rendering in Plot Text Elements — cli_markdown
code: cli_markdown(..., base_family = "", base_size = NULL)

title: Enable Markdown Rendering in Plot Text Elements — cli_markdown
text: ... Character strings naming text elements enable markdown . Supported names: `"title"`, `"subtitle"`, `"caption"`, `"xlab"`, `"ylab"`, `"xtext"`, `"ytext"`, `"axis_title"`, `"axis_text"`, `"legend_title"`, `"legend_text"`, `"strip"`, `""`. empty, enables markdown `"title"`, `"subtitle"`, `"caption"` (common use case). Use `""` enable everywhere. base_family Base font family markdown text. Default `""` uses current theme default. base_size Base font size. `NULL` preserves current theme's size.

title: Build a ggplot2 Scale from a Cliomicplot Palette — palette_scale
code: palette_scale(   palette = "jco",   aesthetic = c("color", "fill"),   type = c("discrete", "continuous"),   alpha = 1,   reverse = FALSE,   ... )  scale_color_cli(palette = "jco", alpha = 1, reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_fill_cli(palette = "jco", alpha = 1, reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_color_cli_c(palette = "blues", alpha = 1, reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_fill_cli_c(palette = "blues", alpha = 1, reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: Build a ggplot2 Scale from a Cliomicplot Palette — palette_scale
text: palette Character. Name palette registry (use `cli_palette_list()` see choices). aesthetic One `"color"` `"fill"`. type One `"discrete"` (default) `"continuous"`.  Continuous works best sequential/diverging palettes. alpha Numeric (0, 1]. Opacity applied colours. reverse Logical. `TRUE`, reverse palette order. ... arguments passed underlying `ggplot2::scale_*_manual()` `ggplot2::scale_*_gradientn()`.

title: Build a ggplot2 Scale from a Cliomicplot Palette — palette_scale
text: ggplot2 Scale object.

title: Clinical Trials Pipeline Stacked Bar Chart — type_trials
text: Creates horizontal stacked bar chart showing pipeline clinical trials, projects, portfolio broken category segments within row. Segments color-coded labelled; total counts appear right.

title: Clinical Trials Pipeline Stacked Bar Chart — type_trials
code: type_trials(   segment_colors = NULL,   segment_text_colors = NULL,   bar_height = 0.72,   segment_border = "#EEE9D4",   segment_border_width = 0.5,   label_threshold = 3,   show_totals = TRUE,   total_nudge = 0.15 )

title: Clinical Trials Pipeline Stacked Bar Chart — type_trials
text: segment_colors Named character vector mapping segment labels fill colours. Defaults active qualitative palette. segment_text_colors Named character vector mapping segment labels label colours text drawn inside segments. Defaults dark--light heuristics. bar_height Height bar fraction row spacing (default 0.72). segment_border Colour border drawn around segment rectangle (default "#EEE9D4"). segment_border_width Line width segment borders (default 0.5). label_threshold Minimum segment width internal label drawn (default 3). show_totals Logical. Whether show total-count label right row (default TRUE). total_nudge Fraction maximum total used padding x-axis limit totals shown (default 0.15).

title: Volcano Plot Type — type_volcano
text: Creates publication-ready volcano plots visualizing   differential expression omics results. Points colored   significance log2 fold-change thresholds.

title: Volcano Plot Type — type_volcano
code: type_volcano(   pval_cutoff = 0.05,   fc_cutoff = 1,   point_size = 2.2,   point_alpha = 0.72,   label_genes = NULL,   max_overlaps = 15,   up_color = "#D73027",   down_color = "#2B6CB0",   ns_color = "#A8B0BA",   cutoff_region_alpha = 0.045,   show_threshold_labels = TRUE )

title: Volcano Plot Type — type_volcano
text: pval_cutoff P-value cutoff significance (default 0.05) fc_cutoff Absolute log2 fold-change cutoff (default 1, .e. 2-fold) point_size Point size (default 2.2) point_alpha Point transparency (default 0.72) label_genes Character vector gene names label, "significant" label significant genes, NULL labels. max_overlaps Maximum overlapping labels ggrepel (default 15) up_color Color upregulated points (default "red") down_color Color downregulated points (default "blue") ns_color Color non-significant points (default "grey70") cutoff_region_alpha Alpha lightly shaded significant regions. show_threshold_labels Add text labels cutoff lines.

title: Volcano Plot Type — type_volcano
text: cliplot_type object use cliplot.

title: Volcano Plot Type — type_volcano
text: Formula orientation matters. cliplot uses standard y ~ x convention, volcano plot puts log2 fold-change x-axis significance y-axis. Supply formula -log10(PValue) ~ logFC (.e. y ~ x significance term left). Row names data used gene labels. Use FDR-adjusted p-value column (e.g. padj) y term available, significance threshold reflects multiple-testing control.

title: Volcano Plot Type — type_volcano
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic volcano plot (y ~ x: significance ~ fold-change) cliplot(-log10(padj) ~ logFC, data = deg_results, type = "volcano")  # With gene labels cliplot(-log10(padj) ~ logFC, data = deg_results,         type = type_volcano(label_genes = "significant"))  # Custom cutoffs cliplot(-log10(padj) ~ logFC, data = deg_results,         type = type_volcano(pval_cutoff = 0.01, fc_cutoff = 1.5)) } # }

title: cliomicplot Session Summary
text: Fixed cliplot() plot type’s preprocessing function runs drawing. Added forwarding range aesthetics xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax, extra_data. Improved base package theme stronger text hierarchy, clearer axes, better panel borders, cleaner legends. Added new showcase theme presentation-ready plots. Updated default qualitative/sequential/diverging palette settings. Improved common plot defaults points, bars, lines, histograms, densities, boxplots, violins. Volcano: shaded significant regions, stronger thresholds, better labels legend visibility. MA: separate /significant colors, clearer log-scale x-axis, improved LOESS styling. PCA: larger outlined points, filled ellipses, optional centroids, clearer labels. Swimmer: converted bars true timeline segments improved event marker rendering. Waterfall: corrected response classification defaults added PD/PR threshold labels. Heatmap: made row/column scaling actually apply improved diverging colors. Correlation: improved tile borders, coefficient contrast, significance handling. Regenerated roxygen documentation man/. Regenerated NAMESPACE. Added required LICENSE file MIT + file LICENSE. Added Author Maintainer. Removed unused RColorBrewer import. Removed LazyData data/ directory. Added build ignores .gitignore, source tarballs, Rplots.pdf.

title: cliomicplot 0.1.0
text: cliplot() plotting engine formula, data.frame, matrix, default methods, built ggplot2. 40+ plot types spanning clinical (volcano, forest, Kaplan-Meier, waterfall, swimmer), omics (heatmap, PCA/MDS, MA, correlation, boxplot, violin), general/creative visualizations. Journal-specific themes (nature, science, nejm, lancet, cell, …) via clitheme(), plus custom theme registration. 55+ curated color palettes, including colorblind-safe options, exposed drop-ggplot2 scales. Built-statistical annotations (stat.test) markdown text rendering.

title: cliomicplot 0.1.0
text: Volcano plots now document correct -log10(p) ~ logFC formula orientation; use FDR-adjusted p-value column significance axis. stat.test emits warning >2-group pairwise comparisons shown without multiple-testing correction, warns (instead silently nothing) x-axis continuous, grouping variable present, fewer two groups exist.
